top_signals.title: Tanker set ablaze after being struck by projectile in the Strait of Hormuz as Iran mourns Khamenei
top_signals.summary: A tanker traveling off the coast of Oman in the Strait of Hormuz caught on fire early Tuesday morning after being struck by a projectile, the British military said. The attack was the latest targeting a vessel moving through the narrow mouth of the Persian Gulf, through which a fifth of all oil and natural ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: d’Amico International Shipping S.A. Announces The Sale Of The Oldest Vessel Of Its Fleet At A Very Attractive Price
top_signals.summary: d’Amico International Shipping S.A., an international marine transportation company operating in the product tanker market, announces that its operating subsidiary d’Amico Tankers d.a.c. top_signals.title: OOCL vessel receives first green methanol stem in Qingdao
top_signals.summary: A dual-fuel container ship owned by Hong Kong-based Orient Overseas Container Line (OOCL) was bunkered with green methanol in a ship-to-ship operation at Qingdao Port in China on 3 July. The 24,000-TEU methanol dual-fuel container vessel, OOCL Wisdom, received 1,500 mt of green methanol, according to state-owned media outlet China Daily. The operation marked OOCL ...

top_signals.title: Statement by President von der Leyen with Taoiseach Martin on the occasion of the College visit to the Irish Presidency

top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Russia tanker operators step in as others in non-G7 fleet retreat amid flag clampdowns
top_signals.summary: Russian tanker operators have increased their crude shipments from home in recent months, as their peers based in some non-G7 countries retreated amid a clampdown from some flag registries. S&P Global Commodities at Sea and Maritime Intelligence Risk Suite data shows tankers operated by Russian companies lifted 18.7 million barrels from Russian ports in June, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Soybeans Rise as China Buys More
top_signals.summary: Soybean futures rose above $11.8 per bushel, hitting a five-week high as renewed Chinese demand for US supplies boosted market sentiment. State-owned trader Cofco reportedly booked at least six cargoes of US soybeans for September-October shipment, adding to the 200,000 metric tons already purchased by Chinese buyers, according to the USDA. The renewed buying comes ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: After “The BHP Files”: What we learned about policy’s role in decarbonising mining
top_signals.summary: While the dust has settled somewhat following the recent media investigation The BHP Files, it is important to recognise and begin applying the key lessons for Western Australia’s Pilbara and beyond. Principally, the context surrounding The BHP Files confirmed that solutions for mining decarbonisation are mostly mature. However, policy evolution is needed for uniform action, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Port Pro of the Month: Beatriz Calzada Ojeda (ES)
top_signals.summary: Can you briefly tell us about Port of Las Palmas highlighting its key features and current challenges? The Port Authority of Las Palmas is a public entity within Spain’s State Ports System, responsible for the management of five ports across the islands of Gran Canaria, Lanzarote and Fuerteventura. Strategically located in the Mid-Atlantic, the Port ...

top_signals.title: India’s coal imports fall 13% in April; power sector imports drop 25%
top_signals.summary: India’s coal imports fell nearly 13 per cent year on year in April 2026, with overseas purchases by the power sector declining by almost a quarter, signalling progress in the government’s efforts to reduce dependence on imported fuel through higher domestic supplies. Total coal imports declined to 21.13 million tonnes (mt) in April from 24.27 ...
